nl: using mtcars, keep only the rows where mpg is greater than 20
generated_code: filter(mtcars, mpg > 20)

nl: from mtcars, select only the mpg and cyl columns
generated_code: mtcars %>% select(mpg, cyl)

nl: using mtcars, create a new column called hp_per_cyl that is hp divided by cyl
generated_code:
mtcars <- mtcars %>% mutate(hp_per_cyl = hp / cyl)
mtcars %>% select(hp, cyl, hp_per_cyl) %>% head()

nl: compute the mean of the mpg column in mtcars
generated_code: mean(mtcars$mpg)

nl: using mtcars, group by the cyl column and compute the mean mpg for each group
generated_code:
mtcars %>%
  group_by(cyl) %>%
  summarise(mean_mpg = mean(mpg))

nl: sort mtcars by mpg in descending order
generated_code: mtcars %>% arrange(desc(mpg))

nl: create two small data frames, a=data.frame(id=1:3, x=c("a","b","c")) and b=data.frame(id=2:4, y=c("x","y","z")), then join them by id keeping all rows from a
expected_code:
a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, x = c("a", "b", "c"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
b <- data.frame(id = 2:4, y = c("x", "y", "z"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
left_join(a, b, by = "id")
generated_code:
a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, x = c("a", "b", "c"))
b <- data.frame(id = 2:4, y = c("x", "y", "z"))
result <- left_join(a, b, by = "id")
result

nl: in mtcars, rename the mpg column to miles_per_gallon
generated_code:
mtcars <- mtcars %>% rename(miles_per_gallon = mpg)
head(mtcars)

nl: from mtcars, extract the first 5 rows
generated_code: mtcars %>% slice(1:5)

nl: from mtcars, return only the rows with unique values in the cyl column
generated_code: mtcars %>% distinct(cyl, .keep_all = TRUE)

nl: count how many cars in mtcars have each number of cylinders
generated_code: mtcars %>% count(cyl)

nl: from mtcars, extract the mpg column as a plain numeric vector
generated_code: mtcars$mpg

nl: using the iris dataset, compute the mean of Sepal.Length and Sepal.Width across all rows
generated_code:
iris %>%
  summarise(
    mean_Sepal_Length = mean(Sepal.Length),
    mean_Sepal_Width  = mean(Sepal.Width)
  )

nl: using mtcars, add a column called mpg_cat that is "high" if mpg is above 25, "medium" if mpg is between 15 and 25, and "low" otherwise
generated_code:
mtcars <- mtcars %>%
  mutate(mpg_cat = case_when(
    mpg > 25 ~ "high",
    mpg >= 15 & mpg <= 25 ~ "medium",
    mpg < 15 ~ "low"
  ))

mtcars %>% select(mpg, mpg_cat) %>% head(10)
table(mtcars$mpg_cat)

nl: using mtcars, add a column called efficient that is TRUE if mpg is greater than 25, FALSE otherwise
generated_code:
mtcars <- mtcars %>%
  mutate(efficient = mpg > 25)

head(mtcars)

nl: create data frames a=data.frame(x=1:2, y=3:4) and b=data.frame(z=5:6, w=7:8), then row-bind them together
expected_code:
a <- data.frame(x = 1:2, y = 3:4)
b <- data.frame(x = 5:6, y = 7:8)
bind_rows(a, b)
generated_code:
a <- data.frame(x = 1:2, y = 3:4)
b <- data.frame(z = 5:6, w = 7:8)
rbind(a, b)
dplyr::bind_rows(a, b)

nl: in mtcars, move the cyl column so it appears as the first column
generated_code:
mtcars %>% relocate(cyl, .before = 1) %>% head()
mtcars <- mtcars %>% relocate(cyl, .before = 1)

nl: from mtcars, extract the top 5 rows with the highest mpg values
generated_code:
mtcars %>%
  slice_max(mpg, n = 5)

nl: create a vector v = c(1, NA, 3, NA, 5) and replace all NA values in it with 0
generated_code:
v <- c(1, NA, 3, NA, 5)
v[is.na(v)] <- 0
v

nl: using mtcars, create a new column prev_mpg that contains the mpg value from the previous row
generated_code:
mtcars <- mtcars %>%
  mutate(prev_mpg = lag(mpg))

head(mtcars[, c("mpg", "prev_mpg")], 10)

nl: using mtcars, make a scatter plot of mpg on the x-axis and wt on the y-axis
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt)) + geom_point()
generated_code:
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt)) +
  geom_point()

nl: using the economics dataset from ggplot2, make a line chart of date on the x-axis and unemploy on the y-axis
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(economics, aes(x = date, y = unemploy)) + geom_line()
generated_code: ggplot(economics, aes(x = date, y = unemploy)) + geom_line()

nl: using mtcars, make a bar chart showing the count of cars for each number of cylinders
generated_code:
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(x = "Cylinders", y = "Count", title = "Count of Cars by Number of Cylinders") +
  theme_minimal()

nl: using mtcars, make a histogram of the mpg values
generated_code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg)) + geom_histogram(bins = 10, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") + labs(title = "Histogram of MPG", x = "Miles per Gallon", y = "Count") + theme_minimal()
